reviews.review_description:
I spent 9.5hrs getting the hang of this game, after realising id made too many mistakes and not leveling up and being careless with my matter, I decided to start over, It didn't take long to progress once I knew what I was doing.

The game is demanding to begin with but once you get past the nuances and remember to go back and offload all your gear, spend or bank all your matter and increase your abilities it becomes more than rewarding. I spent too long trying to get past the Black Knight but in the end I realised my level 2 character wasn't going to cut it, I came very close a few times but in the end the character wasn't strong enough.

I'm only a few hours into the 2nd run but far more experienced and really enjoying it, less of a grind and my character is far more advanced so I feel like I'm better prepared for the boss fights.

Having a blast, great work Devs, Highly recommend if you love swordplay, melee, archery and ranged fighting, still need to grind if you want to advance, but looking forward to unlocking the next areas.

Waited so long for this and what we got is junk..sorry devs. The graphics are mostly terrible and clingy except a few areas. The fighting is laughable. There's little guidance or instructions so you are stuck going round and round trying to figure out how to carry on next stmegment to no avail. Though finally decided that the reward isn't worth my time in this game. Not sure why devs aimed so low from their PC version. But here we are.

reviews.review_title: The greatest melee game in VR
reviews.review_description:
This is the best action adventure experience ￼I’ve had with melee weapons in VR by a long shot.

At first, I had a rough time with this game I admit, but once I got over the learning curve, I was having a blast and everything clicked.

I adore how realistic and detailed yet challenging the combat is. 
The world was fun to explore and I keep thinking back to this game and how happy I was playing through it. it very much reminded me of my first time playing dark souls, and how much I enjoyed persevering through that challenging world.

I do like the haptic feedback from the shield. Combat is fun when your sword and arrows dont phase through the enemies. If you swing too fast your sword will just go right through the enemy and do no damage. Also Devs please fix the player arms. They don't have joints, feel really weird, and ruin the immersion when your entire arm is just a really thick forearm attached to your shoulder. Graphics are terrible too but I dont really care that much about that.(just included in case anyone cares about graphics)

I got this game on sale, and I'd rather rewatch madam web, kraven the hunter, and mobius five times in a row with no brakes, while eating full pineapples. And that's not it, but if I said the rest I'd get arrested, but the thing that truly ticks me is that I can't refund it because I got it on sale

Wow! This game is so good! I don’t remember when last time vr game give me so many emotions and adrenaline haha 

First game was looking a bit boring (but it’s just because I wa a blind to see stairs on a wall! 

So much fun happens after! Feeling a bit cheater self because of mage path but it’s incredible experience :) 

Highly recommend :)

Performance and visuals are definitely worse than they had to be on Quest 3. LODs, and enviroment quality are pretty bad most of the time.
Unfortunately the first few areas are the ugliest and hardest part of the game, it gets better after that tho.

There is some fun to be had and I think it's worth giving it a try if you really like souls likes. 

Took me about 20h to beat but I did take my time.

reviews.review_title: Great game if you know what you R doing
reviews.review_description: I never played a dark souls game and I saw this game and attempted it I quit immediately so a while later I come back after playing Elden ring and I finally understood the mechanics in short great game if you have ever played a souls like before

Buy this game. This game has the most realistic sword fighting combat/physics. It’s not easy to kill and that’s what makes this game so great. You have to hit critical spots and exposed areas on enemies, not just mindlessly swing your arms around. The story mode alone makes this game my favorite. Better than any game I’ve played, VR or not. You really feel like you’re in a medieval fantasy world. The bosses are so exciting to come across you don’t know what’s next. Every boss has been full of surprises and honestly so fun to fight. It’s also cool to find all these pieces for weapons that are scattered across the worlds. I’ve finished the game but started replaying, I was disappointed that I finished it that’s how good this game is. I wonder if I had said no to destroying that last crystal at the end I could have gotten more content? I tried to say no but it didn’t let me select that option. Anyway 10/10 game. Thanks for this masterpiece.

developer_response.body: Thank you for your review. We are truly honored. We remain fully committed to creating a realistic combat adventure game. Please stay tuned, as we will continue to release updates.

reviews.review_description: The physics-based combat is top-tier. Unlike other games where you just waggle your controllers, here every swing and block has a real sense of weight and impact. The haptics are so satisfying when you parry an attack. It’s challenging, it’s demanding, but that’s exactly why it’s so addictive. The environmental design is hauntingly beautiful. If you want a serious challenge with the best sword-and-board mechanics on the Quest, get this now! 🤘🔥

This is a post regarding around the bow/crossbow. I noticed that with the bow you can shoot at the enemies head/body and watch as the arrow quite literally phase through their body. No matter how fast or quick you shoot they keep going through the bodies while only a couple of them actually hits. I don't know if its with my headset or the game itself but with how the arrows go through the enemies bodies without even injuring them is quite frustrating especially when your trying to do those three challenges in Arena story mode. The swinging/stabbing with different weapons going through the enemies body without injuring them is understandable if you go too fast because me and many other users are used to Blade and Sorcery: Nomad or other games like that. So I won't say much about that other than I wish it was adjusted a little. Overall the game is amazing and I love it.

developer_response.body: We are working on resolving this issue. The problem arises because, after enabling AppSW, arrows sometimes miss physics calculations when the frame rate is insufficient, especially at close range.

reviews.review_title: Don't miss it if you love challenge
reviews.review_description: I didn't expect anything from the game but loved it! Gives a great story-driven challenge,  but it is not roguelike! So that you continue from the last checkpoint if you die, enjoying very nice physics-based combats and truly beautiful weapons.

This game is hard as dark souls, and kind of a copy of Elden Ring but very fun you need to add multiplayer. You should make this thing. We like you have magic like the Witcher and I know if there’s already magic staffs, and if you want a ￼ a game, like dark souls, then get this game ￼￼￼￼
Really happy to have bought this. It’s exactly what you would expect from a soulslike. It’s challenging and there’s lots of replay ability. Absolute must if you want value for money

I genuinely couldn't even get far enough into the game to make a longer review because the game was so frustrating. The arms are incredibly ugly and large and stiff, the grabbing is broken, you can grab okay with some jank but then your fingers just shake around, unable to do anything. The height calibration is annoying and confusing, so it the turning settings. You cant enjoy the full game if you make it this unbearable

Way too much dialogue, having to go through five different monologues from 3 people just to be able to move back to an area you already went through. Then most of the gameplay is walking around with xbox 360 style graphics, and the occasional swordfight which is won by swinging at the head. Sword physics are jank and the guardian fight was uninteresting, even with armor destruction, because of a bug that if your sword moved too fast, it didn't hit it.

developer_response.body: Hey, thanks for your feedback. Holding down the trigger button allows you to quickly skip the dialogue box. The weapon-through-the-path bug is still being fixed. Due to the Quest's physics calculation capabilities, we can't completely avoid this at the moment, but this phenomenon will gradually decrease as you switch to larger weapons.

reviews.review_description: So I got this game because I thought it would be like blade and sorcery, and while there are some similarities there are also differences. First the graphics are amazing! They are extremely good. And the zombie models are great! When I think of a zombie the zombie in game is pretty much exactly what I think of. But I got stuck on the 2nd boss, I could not get passed him at all. But all the game before that is a masterpiece. And I didn’t realize until far into the game that you can cut their heads off. Which is gruesome but good, it adds detail! So yeah that’s my rating on this amazing game!

reviews.review_title: It forgot what made the game fun.
reviews.review_description: Starts out as a game that carefully balances armor and weapon design so precise strikes can get past enemy defenses.  A careful arrow shot could bring down an armored enemy if it went through the visor or into the throat.  Ends as a game that forgets everything good about its own design and provides regular enemies with no weak spots, and massive health pools who can soak up over a dozen armor piercing crossbow shots to the face.  Its not the bosses that have made me quit, they are kinda boring to be honest.  What ended it for me is the now dull combat as I blandly smack Royal Guards literally anywhere until they anti-climactically just fall over.  Its not hard, its just tedious and fighting doesnt feel fun.

What starts as a cool Souls-Like VR with cool ideas gradually shows its true ugly face mid-point where bosses throw balance out of the window with attacks you can't see nor anticipate, HP pools way too big, tons of texture and lighting bugs (might be bad optimization for Quest), a crafting system that is really cool but a pain to use and a very underutilized climbing mechanic. I liked my first few hours but can't force myself to finish it.

developer_response.body: Hey, we apologize for the poor gaming experience. We'd love to know exactly what difficulties you encountered. If you'd like to tell us, it might help us adjust the game's details. Alternatively, you can find ways to defeat your enemies in our forums.

reviews.review_description: Awesome game, methodical physics based combat and Dark Souls vibes, if they added coop multiplayer this would be next level

When I start this game I can’t get past the first part. You can see absolutely nothing. Watching people on YouTube? They have no issues with the brightness and the game play looks amazing. The actual gameplay on the headset lacks majorly. I have not found a way to change this in the game settings and the standard brightness in the head does nothing.

developer_response.body: Hey, the scene being too dark is likely due to a contrast adjustment issue in the settings. Please find the contrast setting in your Quest system settings and adjust it to 0.

reviews.review_title: Greatest Action Game Of All Time.
reviews.review_description: If you are looking for a dark souls like game or just want a hard realistic dark fantasy medieval sword fighting you should buy this. The combat is realistic you cant penetrate armor unless you hit armor gaps like their neck. And boss fights are the funnest to play this is currently my favorite game of all time. The only thing is the boss fights need to be much harder it only takes me 2-5 tries max to beat each boss. And I believe this is the future of vr gaming i have not played a game like this and my whole library are just pure action games like boxing underdog, blade and sorcery, battle talent. But this game beats all of them and I will never see these games the same again after playing this. I hope gaming developers take aspiration from this game because this is the greatest game of all time.

developer_response.body: We are deeply honored to receive such high praise from you. While the game still has some shortcomings, we will continue to strive for improvement. Please stay tuned for future updates!

reviews.review_description: Tedious trash that showed promise but became bland, reptitive and uniquely boring. Everything is a damage sponge, magic is overtuned, run-backs are egregiously counter-intuitive. If a boss's theme loops while fighting it you started wrong from the beginning.

developer_response.body: Hey. Thanks for your feedback. When using melee weapons, if you accurately hit the enemy's weak points, you can kill them quickly. For example, pierce their neck or chest. Against zombie enemies, you can also chop off their heads.

reviews.review_description: Im not one with scary themes in vr games, im just wondering if its not that scary, 5 thumbs up if i should play it

developer_response.body:
Hey,
Don't be too nervous; this isn't a horror game. You just need to proceed cautiously. Trust the sword in your hand.
I hope you like it.

reviews.review_description: Its good i guss
I love it but it glitches to much

reviews.review_title: This is What a VR Game Should Be
reviews.review_description: Grimlord" may not have a AAA budget or overwhelming marketing, but it possesses the qualities most scarce in today's VR gaming: purity, solidity, and respect. It respects VR's immersive potential and realizes it with solid physical interaction. It respects the player's intelligence and skill, offering deep challenge and reward. Most importantly, it respects the player's time, repaying your investment with a complete, replayable world.
It’s like Elden ring some of the combat is insanely hard while after you get stronger some of it gets easier
Can someone tell me why evertime i save all your enemies that you killed are all back, and after i bought a load of arrows 120 my headset was dying so I saved and my arrows were back to 30 ?

developer_response.body:
Hey, please allow me to explain the game mechanics to you. First, the enemy resurrection mechanism is borrowed from classic Souls-like games; all enemies except the boss will respawn after each death, rest, or teleportation.

Second, regarding purchasing arrows, since there's no auto-save mechanism in the monastery, if you purchase arrows and don't rest or teleport, the game won't save. Therefore, you need to rest at a teleportation point to save your progress. Your arrows will disappear, but your materials and money will return to their previous state.

reviews.review_title: I died 50 times and loved every second.
reviews.review_description: This is basically "You Died: The Simulator." My arms are sore from swinging giant swords, and I'm terrified of what's around the corner. The bosses wiped the floor with me, but I keep coming back for more. 10/10 would rage quit again.
I played th game and the collision sucked, I hit a guy not even fast and it didn’t detect, also when I block people it doesn’t work. And the graphics are terrible

developer_response.body:
This sounds a bit bad. But we rarely receive feedback like this, and we value your opinions very much. If it's convenient for you, please record a video of the problem and send it to our community. We will find the problem and improve it as soon as possible.

 our discord address 
https://discord.gg/zAKRuU89Ux

reviews.review_description:
When I go into the settings I can't get back out of the settings selection screen when I select the back button to return to main menu.  Tried uninstalling and reinstalling and have the same problem.  When I select back it just turns gray for a second then goes back to white.

Edit, thanks to the developers actually acknowledging and responding to this review and fixing the problem apparently many were having. Just for listening to your customers is worth bumping it up to 4 stars. Gonna check it out when I get home.

Before I start with suggestions, I first want to say that this game is good and has a very good concept I also really like how it is a long game. ￼


    I like the immersive things that they added to the game. Like the finger physics and how you can put a cap on your potion to save it,but they’re missing the one most important thing. ￼the feel to the combat. It doesn’t feel right and sometimes my sword goes though the enemy. Also this game is 30 bucks

developer_response.body: Yes. We read your suggestion. The issue of weapons penetrating enemies has indeed been bothering us for some time, especially when using some of the more delicate weapons. Because we can't maintain a high frame rate on Quest, we've had to use AppSW to reduce latency. This can cause some physics calculation errors. We're still tweaking the engine. In UE5.5, we've reduced the probability of these errors to below 10%, but it's still not completely avoidable. It's really a headache.

reviews.review_description: This is literally Dark Souls in VR, exactly as good as you could imagine. I’ve played so many games in VR, but this is finally the first VR game I’ve played that felt like a GAME! It’s not a sandbox, it’s not an “experience,” it feels like all the console games I love and get lost in. The combat feels so real and takes tact, and the worlds are great. It’s the first time I had things planned irl but said “just a little more, just a little more” and kept playing for hours. THANK YOU devs, THIS is what VR is supposed to be.
Sorry about that, the game is actually really good thank you. 😀

developer_response.body: Hey, I think the first boss you're referring to is the guy with the axe? You can leave through the door behind him. Jump over the drawbridge. This won't affect your progress. Of course, you'll need some patience if you want to defeat him.

reviews.review_description: I am Currently addicted to this game highly recommended to others that enjoy dark souls games and need a challenge.

reviews.review_title: Fairly good game, too short though
reviews.review_description:
Fairly good game, too short though. Also quite repetitive and not hard at all after figuring out a few things. Like armor gaps and hos easy is to block.

Some improvements:
The bell is a joke, hard to interact with and easy to drop potions that are lost easily. Also too small and should show how much life and mana you have when you are drinking the potions.

The combat system should rely on active block because is silly to let the wapon just in front of you and it blocks a strong attack by itself. Buut blocking on your right is really hard, it takes a while to wrist swing the weapon to do so.

Too much clipping and lacking of diseganging actions, like pushes break immersion.

Please make the armor games wider and connect the hit to them, is too sloppy.

developer_response.body: Thank you for your feedback. Our story mode offers at least 10 hours of gameplay, which should surpass most VR games. Perhaps you could give it a try?

reviews.review_description:
Very fun! If you are too young to get Blade and Sorcery, play this. Very similar with graphics and physics. I love thus far! 

Devs:
Please add mod support! it would make it fun. Just in sandbox mode though. Also, please add multiplayer.

I play the PCVR version via Virtual Desktop. The higher resolution and increased level of detail definitely add value to the game, but overall performance could be better. Even on strong GPUs a lot of stuttering occurs. The game is janky, clunky and buggy, BUT it is able to create that mysterious Dark Souls atmosphere. I could not stop playing and it took me about 25 hours to get to the end. What a great experience full of intense moments. Despite the flaws, I strongly recommend this!

Yes the game has some flaws but for the "Like Souls" it gives, I enjoy every minute I play.  
Its fun running through the level section and seeing everything connected. Look at places like, how am I going to get up there or over there.
I play as a melee character but my second run will be as a magic user. 
One thing that does suck is the swinging though enemies but if you slow your attack down there is no issue!

I really hope you guys are building off this game and developing something new and just as good. Grimlord and Behemoth are my 2 games I compare others to!

But when I try to hit sometimes there body is like steel it won't do any damage other than a scratch and there isn't many enemies that exist on the game and they don't fall that much and with armour they are a little bugged
Thanks for reading

developer_response.body: Hey, thanks for your feedback. You can try knocking off their helmets and then piercing their heads. Or use a heavier weapon. If the heavy weapon is two-handed, use both hands to increase its stability. Most enemies will stagger after a few powerful attacks. If you continue your onslaught, they will fall.

reviews.review_title: The greatest game in the world
reviews.review_description: No game could’ve be like that

If you're playing this standalone, keep in mind you're going to just outright have worse time than you would playing it with PCVR. Not saying this as some graphics purist, standalone just struggles with the physics at times, causing issues like your weapon swinging "past" enemies like they're not there, or issues with parrying if you're swinging too fast. If you have the option to play this on PC, do that. You'll enjoy yourself far better

reviews.review_title: Great atmosphere, good story, ok combat
reviews.review_description:
Grimlord has a lot of cool ideas behind it, and you can tell there’s passion behind the world-building and atmosphere. The environments look good, the armor and enemy designs are interesting, and the overall vibe of the game hits that dark fantasy tone really well. When things do connect, the physics-driven combat feels satisfying in theory. There’s a lot of potential here for a very fun melee VR experience.

But the hit detection is honestly rough enough to undermine most of that potential. Unless you move your weapon in slow motion, your swings just phase straight through enemies. The moment you try to slash with any real speed or intent, the game doesn’t register the hit at all. It feels like you’re supposed to gently touch them instead of actually landing blows.

Same issue with the crossbow. I’ve watched bolts pass clean through unarmored enemies as if they weren’t even there, and then ricochet off guys wearing nothing but cloth in the arena challenge. It’s extremely frustrating, especially when the game asks you to complete objectives like “kill 5 enemies with a crossbow” and the weapon just doesn’t work reliably.

Because of this, stabbing ends up being the only consistent way to deal damage. Slashes, swings, and momentum-based attacks feel pointless outside of boss fights, where the hit detection seems to behave slightly better.

It really makes me question how much playtesting went into the combat, because these issues show up in nearly every encounter. The game has a strong core aesthetic and the foundation for great VR melee combat, but the hit detection needs serious fixing before the gameplay can reach its potential. If the devs refine the collision and weapon interaction, this could be something really special. Right now, it’s frustrating more often than fun.

developer_response.body:
Hey, thanks so much for your feedback and kind words—we really appreciate it!  We're pouring our hearts into building a VR game packed with deep RPG elements. As a small studio, we're constantly working to fix the issues you've pointed out.  That weapon-phasing-through-enemies bug you mentioned is definitely a known gremlin we've been chasing. We've rolled out several updates that have improved it a lot, but we're still seeing reports pop up now and then.  We'd absolutely love to have passionate players like you in our community! Jump in, share your experiences, and help us squash this bug for good—every detail makes a huge difference. 
our discord address
https://discord.gg/zAKRuU89Ux

reviews.review_description: I grab the satchel and no brightness settings it’s fricking dark asf😭

developer_response.body: Hey, the scene being too dark is likely due to a contrast adjustment issue in the settings. Please find the contrast setting in your Quest system settings and adjust it to 0.

reviews.review_title: Buy soap cause you're gonna sweat!
reviews.review_description: I have played this game since the Beta version for 195hrs with 9 different character builds (most played through twice). Worth the money. Responsive programmers. Constant updates (arena mode is one). Lots of weapon crafting and skill options. Good physics. Not perfect but closer to a 5 than a 4. Forget beat saber and beat enemies!

What a half-baked piece of garbage. I'm so glad I didn't pay full price for this crap.

Also: "body type" instead of male and female. A major sign of an ideologically captured studio committed to churning out DEI slop. Avoid.

developer_response.body: Hello, we're sorry the game didn't meet your expectations. We'd also like to understand some details.

reviews.review_title: The Gold Standard for VR Physics Combat
reviews.review_description:
​I’ve played a lot of VR action titles, but the sword-and-shield mechanics in Grimlord are on another level. The haptic feedback when you block a heavy strike is incredibly realistic—you can actually feel the impact through the controllers. It truly makes you feel like a warrior struggling to survive in a brutal world.
​The combat isn't just mindless swinging; it requires genuine skill. You have to study enemy patterns and time your counters, much like the Souls series. The attention to detail on the equipment—from the metallic clink of the blades to the heft of the shield—is masterfully done. This is the dark fantasy immersion I've been waiting for!

reviews.review_title: climbing I figured out, but bug in game
reviews.review_description:
I am so sorry I don't know what's wrong with this app, I re-edited my review a while ago and stated that I figured out the climbing thing. It was very very difficult but, I enjoyed it. 
I think you definitely have a bug though. whenever I build a sword with any kind of gem or fancy handle it doesn't work. The blacksmith will make it and then when he displays it it will show literally just the guard and nothing else and you can't pick it up.

developer_response.body:
Yes, there is a bug with the new sword, and it will be fixed soon. 

You can use your weapon to insert into the planks to climb. Once you reach the edge, grab the edge of the plank and you'll be lifted to the top plane. In most cases, these planks are not essential to the mission.


That's great! We wish you all the best on your gaming journey. Please feel free to contact us if you have any questions, or join our community.

We apologize for the issue you encountered, but it's a very rare occurrence. Did you use the King's Gaze? That helps reduce the difficulty of the boss fight.

reviews.review_description:
i was very excited with this game but unfortunately 
enemy swarm you all the time and what it should be a nice challenging game it becomes boring and bloated i wish the developers will add difficulty levels so other players can enjoy the game but in this state i cannot play it anymore

developer_response.body: We apologize for the less-than-pleasant experience. We recommend you exercise caution when exploring the map. Admittedly, we could add a difficulty option to make combat easier, but that would make the game tedious much faster. You can quickly kill enemies by striking vital points, or use blunt weapons to attack heavily armored enemies, which will make combat more efficient.

reviews.review_description: I was near the end of the game in story mode, but I opened the sandbox mode once and now I can't enter story mode again! If I click story and try to go to my save, I always get redirected to sandbox. On Meta Quest 3s

developer_response.body: Hello! We are very sorry about the problem you're experiencing. We take this issue very seriously, and we've seen others mention it in our Discord community, but it seems to be resolvable. If you're worried about losing your save data, you can try using cloud saves to recover your progress.  Please avoid entering sandbox mode for now, and then provide more information in our community so we can help you resolve this issue.

reviews.review_description: Love the Bloodborne game. Hope they make another where it’s not people but full blown scary monsters. The combat is clunky at times or things get stuck here and their it’s a vr game. Just know if you buy this game it isn’t a fast paced hack and slash. Shots have to land and damage done to you is exceptionally high so get skilled and don’t get hit and for bosses it can feel unrealistic since your just swinging away as fast as possible.

This is my favorite game i have on my Quest. It has tons of mechanics and has that slight horror aspect to it. There are a few bugs though, slashing is a big buggy and there is not many gore or blood mechanics to it. Not really a bug but i might as well put it here, sandbox is hard to understand and i still cant quite figure it out, not a complaint just need help understanding it! I would appreciate if a dev could respond and help me out a bit, but anyways i highly recommend it!

developer_response.body:
First of all, thank you for liking our game.

In Sandbox mode, you don't need any save data to enter. Choose the type of enemies generated from the notice board on the arena map. Fight them and simply experience the thrill of combat. In Sandbox mode, you don't need to worry about money or materials. All weapons are free. If you have a story mode save file, all weapon skills unlocked in story mode will automatically carry over to Sandbox mode.

reviews.review_description: Lighting is so dark you can not see where you are going and no option to make it brighter. Even with googles on top brightness, it is like playing at night with no moon or any light. Truly awful waste of my time.

developer_response.body: Hello, you can check your Quest's contrast settings. Generally, adjusting the contrast to the lowest setting should be sufficient to see most scenes clearly.

reviews.review_description: This game is absolutely trash the collision is horrible.I've put a dagger inside of an Archer's head.Entirely and he didn't die anybody who is saying that this is a good souls like game for v r is lying to you if you want to try it out , I would suggest getting the horizon plus that's only 7.99 rather than buying the game for thirty dollars at least then , you'll have access to a bunch of cool games this game has potential and I don't know if they're updating it still if they are , that's crazy to me , because i've seen this game on here for a while , and it's still looks like it's an early access , just be careful before you buy it
Game is pretty solid to be honest. Having a great time with it feels just like playing dark souls enemies can be hard and tricky. Lots of different weapons crafting system overall enjoying. ￼

The game looks good and looks fun but there are a few things ruining it for me.
1. Arms are angled too high like several inches above you real arms and looks weird. Had to shut it off but now I can't kick but enemies sure do?
2. Matter dissapears. I had 2k matter then saved then next I played it was 150. Where did it go?
3. Swordplay has potential but if you've played blade and sorcery then it doesn't feel real and if it doesn't feel real id rather it be more satisfying. I can't tell if I'm damaging them or bouncing off their armor.
4. When you die you lose any used items but all enemies respawn. This makes it unbalanced when you're out of health.  Especially when you get overwhelmed with enemies to begin with.
5. Enemies tend to crowd into me making blocking awkward. 

Dropped another star cuz you lose all matter when you die and since you die alot since every enemies on the battlefield swarms you there is no progression. I just use up all my bombs and health and jump back in to kill the same enemies as before just to try an get enough matter to buy one level up point only to die again and do it all over again. Frustration isn't fun gaming.

Edit: I want to add that the difficulty is not the problem it's why I find it difficult. Examples include. Enemies 
Ragdolling but not dead leading to backstabbing. Weapons caught on enemies while theirs pass through each other without friendly fire. Staves not activating in a consistent manner. Projectiles bypassing shields. Not much damage difference from armor hit and an ax to the face (I mean it's wack em till they die, not much for strategy efficiency) also magic is useless on armored just as much.

developer_response.body: Hey. I see you changed your game review. Losing all your materials is a classic penalty mechanic in Soul-like games, designed to make you more careful and focused during exploration. However, you only lose matter (in-game currency); all the blueprints you find and shortcuts you unlock will be retained. We again suggest you join our community to participate in the discussion so we can better address your questions.

reviews.review_description: I don't know how this game can have a good rating. I was looking forward to it, but when I downloaded it, I saw that the graphics weren't anything special, and the game kept crashing and stuttering. In short, a very bad experience. I uninstalled it after 20 minutes, and I hadn't given it much time.

developer_response.body:
Hello, I have received your feedback. In most cases, our game's crash rate is around 6%. We suggest you provide your system version and device model, as this will help us troubleshoot the issue. Generally, the performance on Quest3 is significantly better than on Quest2. If you would like, please join our Discord group; we will help you resolve the problem.
Our Discord address
https://discord.gg/zrgWB6bewH

reviews.review_description: The dark fantasy world is stunningly crafted, dripping with atmosphere. But the real star is the physics engine. Weapons have distinct weight, and parrying feels tactile and real. The magic system looks cool and integrates smoothly with melee combat. Fighting through the map to reach the bosses feels like a grim, epic journey.

I absolutely love the moment my shield catches an enemy's attack! That hardcore physical feedback makes the combat feel so visceral and good. The only minor issue: when my sword hits an enemy, it could feel a bit more fluid. Right now, the impact feels a little "rough" or resistant in a way that breaks immersion slightly. Hope the hit physics get polished!

Why am I so tall? I'm like 9 feet tall. I feel like Gandalf in Bilbo's house. I tried adjusting the height and I can't get it to fit. I'm either too tall or I can see the top of the character's head. Tried sit/stand. Nothing works. Plus there are no options to customize controls. I don't like where the jump button is. ☹️

developer_response.body: This could be a system height calibration issue. You can try readjusting the ground and height settings in the system. Alternatively, you can choose to play in a seated position.

reviews.review_description:
Compared to other games that also primarily focus on only combat, this game was engaging both in combat and in the story.

There's reason to replay it over and over in order to get everything but it's not required and I like that about grimlord. 

My *main* issue is weapons tend to phase through during combat, but it didn't happen during critical moments. And sometimes weapons pierce armored opponents when they shouldn't, and it doesn't register this correctly as they will topple over without effects.

Other than this, the game was good and I had fun replaying it once or twice. I see myself coming back every now and then to replay the story.

It is indeed fun and it helps that I love dark souls. 
But as much as I love this game the combat is very clunky. If the combat got changed I would rate 5 but it took away from at least my experience. but if you don't mind clunky melee combat go for it.

Although I die a lot (it's tough!), the game is seriously addictive. VR gives this hardcore gameplay unmatched immersion. When you are physically holding the weapon and facing down skeletons and knights, the tension and challenge are unlike anything else.

reviews.review_title: Beautiful amazing game, but a few bugs
reviews.review_description: Very amazing game it’s everything I could have ever asked for in a vr game. It has weapon customability I’ve never seen in a vr game before.The map is beautiful and the controls are really immersive. I love the magic system aswell when you have to draw out your power to get it to shoot. I just got to the mansion part so I’m not too far.  But ever since the new update amulets don’t seem to work at all. I try to equip it and it either floats in the air or goes directly into my bag so I wasn’t able to use my amulet to lower the boss difficulty. And other amulets don’t seem to work either. Other than that I have no issues and I hope you guys aren’t finished updating the game! I’ve also noticed that in some parts of the map the zombie guys just die instantly and I get the souls for it. I mainly noticed it in the final boss of the castle right before you entered his room but it’s not something I’m complaining about haha
Combat is fun but game feels clunky. Some features don't work properly and make for annoying gameplay.

developer_response.body: Hello, I'm sorry for the poor experience. Could you please describe the problem you encountered in more detail?

reviews.review_description: The opening cinematic is epic and instantly pulls you into this dark world. The overall mood is fantastic, but I feel the visual impact of the effects is a bit weak. If that gets improved, the experience would be perfect. Can't wait for the updates!

Horrible graphics, this games finger mechanics are cool but everything else kinda sucks. And the mechanic where you stab your blade into the wood and it helps you climb, doesn’t work for me. The blade will just glitch inside of the wood so i cant play further in the game. Thank you devs for reaching out but neither of those have worked. Honestly the storyline is pretty good. Its just the laggy side that kinda pisses me off

developer_response.body:
Hey. The game's graphics are based on a realistic style. It might not be the best among similar products, but we had to make some sacrifices for a larger map. Regarding climbing planks, perhaps switching to a dagger would make it easier. Or you could shoot some arrows to use as climbing points.

Editor:
Yes, if you are interested, please join our community. We are very happy to listen to the voices of our players.
Discord address is:
https://discord.gg/dq7r2jyt5F

reviews.review_description: Idea is great but the game itself has horrendous hitboxes and crashes whenever more is happening at the same time.

developer_response.body:
Hello, we are very concerned about the crashing issue you mentioned.  According to our backend data, our general crash rate is approximately 4-5%. The problem you described seems more severe than this, and we would like to help you resolve it. If you are willing, we would appreciate it if you could update your review with more information, or join our community. This is very important to us. Thank you!

Discord address is:
https://discord.gg/dq7r2jyt5F

reviews.review_title: Seemed jank at first but it’s actually
reviews.review_description: I really like this game. However need to find tune the combat more. The swords and arrows don’t register sometimes which is annoying. The magic makes the game even more fun. I also enjoy all of the bosses!

developer_response.body: Yes, on Quest, the physics sometimes miss hitboxes due to frame rate issues. However, we are already working on improving this. In the next version, we will upgrade the Unreal Engine version, which may help us resolve these kinds of problems.

reviews.review_description:
Spells are interesting on paper. They require drawing a rune to cast the basic spell and a wide swing motion to cast a higher tier spell. The problem is that it's finicky. Sometimes you'll wide swing and nothing will happen. Sometimes you'll snap turn and it will automatically cast the high tier spell. Sometimes you'll be running around trying to get the higher tier magic to cast repeating the same motion and getting nothing(plenty of mana). Sometimes you'll cast said high tier spell and it will visually hit but will do no damage or status effect just visual effects and consume mana. The only tip I can give to increase the consistency is DO NOT reposition your hands on the staff or use two hands. In fact, just get the scepter rod as fast as you can. It is extremely reliable in activating the higher tier spells and doesn't have the issue of the bottom colliding into your character mesh or the environment and it's faster to use because of the weight system.

General balance. Melee is atrocious. Not necessarily because it feels unplayable, it does have issues, but because of the scaling. Scaling in this game for melee just doesn't exist. I've gone back to the beginning with a level 40 character maxing str (which is supposed to scale physical damage) and a +6 endgame melee weapon and it still will not one shot basic mobs. While magic shares a similar issue, magic has a few massive upsides over melee. You can melt mobs, bypass shields (aim for feet), you gain ranged advantage, YOU CAN DUAL CAST to double your DPS and magic doesn't have the collision issues melee has. (Swing to fast and it passes through an enemy dealing no DMG. Too slow and you do not DMG)

Storage at the hub is decent. Drop it in the chest next to the goblin and to get it back scroll down a scroll and grip the text of the item you want to spawn back. Decent enough. The problem is that it sometimes bugs and doesn't allow you to retrieve your items. This requires closing and restarting the game to fix.

Haven't touched the Arena since the first mission(completed it) because it didn't give any Matter(souls/salt currency) which kills any interest I had in a mode like that, which is to use it to farm. The best farming spot (during this event) I've found was in the Chapter 2 courtyard. Go right into the area with three enemies and continue forward to the Turkey boi next to the Gladiator. Circle back into the room with the other three and group them up and burst all three down with magic. Before NG+ you gain 950 Matter each time you nuke that group. It takes less than a minute each rotation so it doesn't take TOO long to hit max level but it will get monotonous after hearing the dialogue again and again so maybe turn on music. The cost to level caps at 10k.

Edit: Completed Arena. It grants a lot of the upgrade mat that costs 5k. Each Arena trial has 3 objectives. Win, kill x number with X and complete it within X time. The first grants 1 upgrade resource. The second grants 2 and the third grants 4. So each trial can grant 7 upgrade resources which saves you 35k Matter. (or 17.5k during this event) Unfortunately, these rewards do NOT reset upon NG+ so at some point you will have to grind currency to buy what you need to upgrade your gear.

Crafting is 'fair'. Upgrades feel insignificant though(ex:42 to 44 magic damage on a t2+1 staff gem, 44 to 46 on +2, 46 to 48 on +3 and so on), especially given the time it takes to get the material for the upgrades. Each upgrade increases the rare material required by +2 (1, 3, 5, 7, 9, 11 etc). Said rare material costs 5,000 Matter each so have fun with that. The only saving grace is that you get all of your materials back through salvage(except Matter of which only gives a small portion back). The customization is a nice touch but there are clear parts that are the BiS depending on the weapon build you are going for.

Bosses are 'ok'. Chapter 1 was underwhelming but fortunately Chapter 2 steps it up. If you have difficulty in chapter 1 then you may be in for a bit of trouble in the second. This trend continues into the final chapter with the last boss being the most challenging, though it is mostly because of his cheap one shot grab and absurd health pool. If he grabs you then you have a short window of time to stagger him. If you don't then you die. If you already staggered him or his bar isn't close to full then you are just screwed. Enjoy fighting him again. Honestly, he has the worst design of all the bosses simply because of that one shot grab. While others may be absurdly simple, this one, while well designed in concept is ruined by poor balancing. 

Side note: I'm not a fan of bosses having no CD on summoning adds though. Really not a fan of that as a 'difficulty mechanic' as it mostly just causes extra lag and draws out fights unnecessarily. It especially sucks for casters and archers who have finite resources and the bosses themselves are already quite the damage sponges.

Loot. Loot is spread throughout the game but most of it is definitely in the final parts. With all of the non-starter armors being locked behind chapter 2 and beyond. The top tier armors are of course postgame. Enemy item drops are rare. Incredibly rare. You could and probably will go through the prologue to the final chapter and only see a few drops from non-boss enemies with most of those drops either coming from enemies in the final chapter (that have unique item drops) or the Turkey event character.

NG+. After you beat the final boss you gain an item that you can turn in to one of two NPCs. Once you do you gain some dialogue, some equipment depending on who you give it to, a cutscene, credits and an immediate start into NG+. NG+1 grants +20% increased Matter from kills. NG+2 grants an additional 22% (42% total) increased Matter gained. Each NG+ also increases the amount of skill points you can gain through levels. (+10 each NG+)

Outside of that, it has the basics of a souls game down. The enemy AI isn't awful but they aren't anything special either. They have set boundaries so it's easy to abuse them by kiting them to the edge of their boundary where they can't push forward and will just eat damage. You can also fairly easily bait enemies into falling to their death from ladders. Some bosses, particularly in the early game, can also be cheesed. A few in the end game can as well with the right build.

Also, the +10% Matter gained doesn't give +10%. It gives +4.8%. Please fix this. Also, consider changing the text in the skill tree for Vitality and Mana to what they actually grant. The text says 1, 3 and 5 but they actually grant 10, 30 and 50 respectively. I actively wanted to avoid those nodes until I found that out.

I died at least 100 times and I’d still give this 5 star for an appropriately challenging  game. It’s a well done game - combat is great, varied weapons, lots of spells (weapons with magic ability is a cool perk) — long game with plenty of content and types of enemies. 

Maybe it’s just me but I wish I knew from the beginning that potions aren’t one time use - don’t throw them away! They refill on save/death.
I am a massive Dark Souls fan and this is the closest VR tribute. I am on my second playthrough and there will be a third. Excellent job by the devs.

has a glitch where you start the game off permanently crouching. Height and setting are inputted correctly, but despite restarted and remaking and everything it doesn’t work. waste of money and obviously mentally disabled devs

developer_response.body:
Hey, we've provided a quick method to check the issue you raised.
There are two situations in which you might be in a crouching position: 
1. You are in a seated gaming position, and you push the right joystick downwards (if your dominant hand for movement is your left hand). In this case, you will remain in a crouching position. To return to a standing position, push the right joystick upwards. 
2. If you are in a standing position, you need to check if the ground settings in your Quest system are correct. To verify this, click "Auto" in the settings to perform height calibration. The data field below will then display your height in centimeters. If this value is incorrect, it means the system calibration needs to be reset.
If you are still experiencing problems, please join our community and provide us with more details. We will help you resolve the issue as soon as possible to ensure you have the best gaming experience.
 our discord address 
https://discord.gg/zAKRuU89Ux

reviews.review_description: It can prove challenging but it's repetitive and boring and it can be slightly interesting but easily boring early in the game

He says It has a portuguese translation, but that is a lie. They could at least use ChatGPT to translate - It would be better than deceiving people. They put a Spanish translation instead of Portuguese. And there até not confort options since you can only move with joystick, which causes náuseas. That is mas o request refund.

I originally got this game ￼hoping for it to be like dark souls and I was right it is as good if not better than dark souls have played all souls games and this is a true vr souls experience 10/10 will you guys add dlc?

developer_response.body: Thank you for playing our game. We do have plans for DLC content.

reviews.review_description: Horrible game, horrible graphics, horrible physics. The game itself is just absolute garbage.

reviews.review_title: The Vr experience I didn’t know I needed
reviews.review_description: It’s a souls like Vr game no doubt but it has exceeded any expectations I had for it. Combat is legit fun and so immersive I’ve had to much fun just farming the first level with different weapons. Highly recommend for any sword and board kind of gamers.

developer_response.body: Thank you for enjoying our game. We will continue to update it and provide more fun content.
